channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Abandonded car, 13th March
item.description:
I reported this car over a year ago and can see someone else has reported this as well. This car has not moved since I last reported it. it cannot move as it has four flat tyres, no exhaust and no steering wheel! It is not roadworthy and as such should not be on public roads
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Homefield Road, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overhanging fir, 13th March
item.description:
Low growing hedge overhanging property boundaries by about 50cm

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Arnos Grove, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Overhanging the highway from private property

item.title: Ivy and plant growth extending onto pavement, 13th March
item.description:
Ivy and ground cover plants are creeping onto the pavement well beyond the boundary of the property boundary. Hasn’t been cleared for a very long time and he’s also collecting litter.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): High Street, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vehicle abandoned, 13th March
item.description:
The vehicle has been parked in our private parking space for over three months and has not moved. Its tax and MOT have expired, and several tickets have already been issued. We are currently unable to use our parking space because of it.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Belfast Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped building waste, 13th March
item.description:
Dumped building waste blocking pavement for wheelchairs /prams

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Seacourt Road, Greater London

item.title: Streatley Rd pavement, 13th March
item.description:
Streatley Rd is filthy with litter one day after waste collection.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Streatley Road, London

item.title: Dumped items, 13th March
item.description:
Dumped items on footpath

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Edmund Road, Welling, Greater London

item.title: Blue bag of rubbish has been dumped for almost 3 weeks, 13th March
item.description:
It needs to be removed. Its 3 weeks now

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 22, The Avenue, St Paul's Cray, Greater London

item.title: Crate, 13th March
item.description:
Pallet has been left on Tressillian Rd for a couple of weeks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 28, Tressillian Road, Brockley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: General waste and flytipping, 13th March
item.description:
I reported this earlier and some of the rubbish was taken away. The whole area needs to cleared and bins emptied. Refuse/recycling collectors do not appear to be emptying the bins.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lancaster Street, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Someone has cut the lock on the fire access gate at junction of Sussex Way & Tollington Road N7 to drive through, 13th March
item.description:
Someone has cut the lock on the fire access gate at junction of Sussex Way & Tollington Road N7 to use it as a short cut. Please replace the lock.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sussex Way, Holloway, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 deep potholes, one off Albion road bexleyhath and one on west street, 13th March
item.description:
Potholes blew up my tyre
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): West Street, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Urgent: missing sign affecting disabled residents’ access, 13th March
item.description:
The missing sign for Maria Close 1–3 has now become a critical issue. This ongoing failure is having a particular impact on disabled residents, who are placed at a substantial disadvantage because they cannot go elsewhere to collect items misdelivered as a result of the missing sign. It has been absent for approximately six months, and during that time our packages have repeatedly been delivered elsewhere. This is having a serious impact on the residents. There are disabled people living at the property who cannot simply go to other addresses or locations to retrieve misdelivered parcels. This means the missing sign is affecting access to deliveries in a way that is particularly serious and unacceptable. Given the length of time this issue has been ongoing and the impact on disabled residents, I ask that this be treated as a matter of urgency and resolved immediately.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Maria Close, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Charlton street outside the rocket, 13th March
item.description:
Door off

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Chalton Street, Somers Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A bicycle frame without wheels tied to a post, 13th March
item.description:
A bicycle frame with sharp edges tied to a post and floating on the sidewalk, posing a serious obstacle and risk For a long time
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Clapton Common, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Couches dumped on public land by 192, 13th March
item.description:
Couches dumped on public land for months. Please can this be cleared.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Eltham Palace Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car unmoved for months filled with bags, 13th March
item.description:
This car has been in the same space for months filled with various bags in the front seat and back, flies are starting to gather outside and inside the car which makes me think something is rotting. It’s taking up a valuable parking space on a residential road

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Horn Lane, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Critical flytipping impacting disabled residents, 13th March
item.description:
Further to my previous report, this situation is now critical. A bag of concrete and a door have been left in the shed at number 3, which means the residents are unable to use it. This is not a minor inconvenience. The shed is needed to store essential mobility equipment. There are two disabled members of the household and, because there are steps, we cannot simply bring mobility equipment into the property itself. The loss of access to the shed is therefore having a direct impact on disabled residents’ day-to-day mobility and independence. I have also not been kept informed of any progress, despite the fact that this issue has become urgent. Given the circumstances, this raises serious concerns not only about trespass and fly-tipping, but also about the impact on disabled residents. This situation may place them at a substantial disadvantage and may amount to a failure to make reasonable adjustments under the Equality Act 2010. Please treat this as an urgent matter, arrange for the immediate removal of these items, and provide me with a clear update on what action has been taken and when the shed will be restored for use. Given the impact on two disabled residents, I ask that this com
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Maria Close, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Two items, 13th March
item.description:
Looks like discarded kitchen appliances.

item.title: Mattress, 13th March

Mattress
